intent: An AI-driven adaptive learning ecosystem that dynamically adjusts curriculum difficulty based on real-time learner sentiment, performance analytics, and cognitive load tracking. Unlike static LMS platforms, CogniPath utilizes a 'Skill-Graph' architecture to suggest personalized micro-learning paths that bridge individual knowledge gaps, focusing on long-term retention rather than module completion.

entities.name: CognitiveStateVector
entities.description: A high-dimensional data object representing a learner's current focus, stress markers, and reaction latency derived from telemetry and interaction patterns.

entities.name: SkillGraphNode
entities.description: A granular representation of a specific competency or knowledge unit, mapping dependencies and prerequisite relationships within the adaptive curriculum.

entities.name: LearningPathOrchestrator
entities.description: The central decision engine that consumes state vectors and performance data to dynamically recalculate the optimal pedagogical trajectory for the user.

entities.name: ComplianceMiddleware
entities.description: A privacy-preserving layer that tokenizes and anonymizes PII from LTI-integrated identity providers before passing telemetry data to the AI processing cluster.

entities.name: ContentInteroperabilityLayer
entities.description: An ingestion adapter that translates SCORM, xAPI, and H5P packages into standardized JSON schemas compatible with the Skill-Graph architecture.

entities.name: AdaptiveTelemetryStream
entities.description: A high-concurrency event bus that captures real-time engagement markers and feeds them into the predictive analytics pipeline.

entities.name: RetentionLedger
entities.description: A persistent audit store that tracks long-term competency mastery over time, functioning as the source of truth for the system's efficacy metrics.

architecture.description: The system architecture utilizes a reactive event-driven flow where learners authenticate via LTI 1.3 standards. Upon login, the ComplianceMiddleware anonymizes identity signals, passing authorization tokens to the ContentInteroperabilityLayer to pull legacy material. As a user engages, the AdaptiveTelemetryStream captures interaction patterns, which are mapped to a CognitiveStateVector. This vector serves as an input for the LearningPathOrchestrator, which traverses the SkillGraphNodes to generate a real-time, non-linear path. The system dynamically injects content adjustments based on the gap analysis between the current SkillGraphNode state and the user's historical proficiency stored in the RetentionLedger, ensuring all AI-driven insights are based on sanitized, FERPA-compliant data streams.

endpoints.summary: Registers an LTI user and returns an anonymized token.
Retrieves the full skill dependency graph.
Ingests real-time engagement markers into the telemetry stream.
Fetches the latest cognitive state vector for a user.
validation_rules: token must be a valid anonymized identifier

endpoints.summary: Retrieves the dynamically calculated learning trajectory.
validation_rules: token must exist in orchestrator records

endpoints.summary: Queries the long-term competency mastery for a specific learner.
Translates external content packages into Skill-Graph compatible JSON.

components.content: Visualizes CognitiveStateVector data for the user.
Displays recent entries from the AdaptiveTelemetryStream.
Displays RetentionLedger data for long-term competency mastery.
Interface for submitting content packages to the system.
Interface for generating anonymized tokens via the UserAnonymizer.

rules.description: User identity must be anonymized via UserAnonymizer before data ingestion.
rules.logic: Incoming telemetry and cognitive state payloads must reference a valid anonymized_token existing in the UserAnonymizer table; payloads failing token validation must be rejected to prevent data leakage.

rules.description: Maintain state transition integrity between CognitiveStateVector and LearningPathOrchestrator.
rules.logic: The LearningPathOrchestrator must validate state vectors against defined calibration thresholds before recalculating pedagogical trajectories.

rules.description: Enforce strict schema validation for interoperable content against the SkillGraphNode table.
rules.logic: Content ingestion processes must map legacy formats to the SkillGraphNode schema; any content missing required metadata fields must be flagged as 'Unprocessable' and excluded from the graph traversal.

rules.description: Ensure RetentionLedger consistency with SkillGraphNode mastery updates.
rules.logic: A proficiency update in the RetentionLedger must trigger only if the LearningPathOrchestrator confirms successful completion of the target SkillGraphNode; prevent duplicate or orphaned ledger entries.

rules.description: Validate pedagogical progression against dependency constraints.
rules.logic: LearningPathOrchestrator must verify that all prerequisite SkillDependency records are satisfied by mastery_level scores in the RetentionLedger before assigning new SkillGraphNodes.
